title: show unified output details
description: in the unified outputs list box on the upper-half of the screen, split it in half. in the lefthand side should be the existing list of displays+highlight; the new right side box will contain details of the output. Added build_output_details() helper function to format output information.

This input state-machine would then be referenced in all rendering components to show highlight state for a child element or to show/hide self, and so on.

title: Add popup help screen tied to ?
description: Add a global keybinding, similar to 'q to quit', to open a popup in the center of the screen that lists all of the current controls. it should be written so that more help commands/explanations can be added easily in the future. The ? key should make a the popup appear, and pressing any key afterward will make the popup disapear. For this ticket, the help popup should show all of the currently bound input keys and what they are tied to. Repeats of the same action for different keys should be grouped together (e.g. j+s+down for 'down')
close_reason: Implemented help popup with ? keybinding. Added HelpPopup state variant, input handling for toggle, and centered popup rendering with grouped keybindings. Code passes cargo check, clippy, and fmt.

title: validate kanshi config

Write tests with example file content, similar to what's in the README.md, that validates that profiles have matching delimeters and content only expected parameters with value content.
close_reason: Completed: Validation logic implemented with comprehensive tests

title: Manual text edit mode for kanshig profiles
description: A new keybinding, e, will be added to the application. When it is pressed, it will open a modal popup, that takes up most of the screen, containing an editable textarea with the contents of the current kanshig file that was loaded (whether default or custom). This change will involve adding a dependency on the 'tui-textarea' rust crate, which will provide this functionality. The crate will be set up to work with ratatui out of the box, so it shouldn't need custom features. The user will have a vanilla textarea to edit the configuration file with. They can press CTRL+s to SAVE the current contents of the textarea to the config file location, overwriting it. After saving, return the user to the main view of the TUI application. The user can also press CTRL+d to Discard Changes to the config file, closing the textarea and returning the main view of the TUI application. The save/discard keybinds should be added to the help screen, alongside the new 'e' key, noting that save/discard only work while editing the config file.

comments.author: claude-code
comments.text: Starting work on manual text edit mode. Need to: 1) Add tui-textarea dependency to Cargo.toml, 2) Add 'e' keybinding to open editor modal, 3) Implement CTRL+s to save and CTRL+d to discard, 4) Update help screen with new keybindings.

title: Create kanshig crate and CLI parsing per design
description: Read the README.md file and then the KANSHIG-PLAN.md file. Following the instructions in the file, DO ONLY: create a new rust workspace and workspace sub-crate named 'kanshig-cli'. It be a cargo --bin project. It can be located at kanshig-cli/ within the root of the repository. It should have idiomatic rust cli flag parsing code for the flag specified in the plan document, the -c / --config flag. The program should be able to read and load the kanshi config file from the filesystem based on whether a flag was provided. It does not need to do anything else at this time
close_reason: Completed: Created kanshig-cli crate with CLI argument parsing for -c/--config flag

title: add output to config: command to add detected-only display to kanshig config as new output
description: We will add a new keybinding to kanshig. When a user has the outputs list focused, and presses 'a', then we check to see if that output exists in the kanshig config. If it is already in the kanshig, we display a message to indicate that this output is already added. but when an entry in the unified output list exists ONLY from the niri msg outputs data, that means it hasn't been added to the kanshig config yet. If a unified output is detected-only, then we can use the 'add output to config' feature to add it. When the key is pressed, a new Add Output window will popup up with a series of single-line textarea inputs, and Add + Cancel buttons, where we can add information about the output. The textareas should have default entries for each piece of data. The texareas are: mode (resolution and refresh rate; use the preferred resolution+refresh niri-output as the default value), position (two numbers, separated by a comma; Have 0,0 be the default), scale (a floating point number; 1.0 is the default), and alias (should be all caps and start with a dollar sign;  is the default). A new rust struct, named AddOutputWindowState will capture the contents of each textarea, along with which item is focused (this can be an enum with numeric backing; 0, 1, 2, 3 are the text areas, 4 is the Add button and 5 is the Cancel button). In the new Add Output window, there will be an Add and Cancel button at the bottom of the window that the user can tab to by going through the text areas. The application, through input tracking, should track which part of the Add Output window is focused/highlighted, similiar to how the output & profile lists are handled.

title: BUG: strange outputs highlight behavior + can't select detected-only outputs
description: Currently, in the upper box that shows the unified list of outputs, if an entry is detected+configured (i.e. it appears in both output lists), it can't be selected/highlighted in the TUI. There is also strange behavior with the state of the output box when switching BACK from the profiles box. It is based, in the human's setup with three outputs (one detected+configured) and three profiles, on weird behavior when jumping back and forth. Also as mentioned above the third entry for the detected + configured display can't be highlighted UNLESS one switches back from the third choice in the profiles window. Something is wonky with the KanshigTuiState variants and how they swap data back and forth. The bug is probably there in that input handling + output counter selecting code

comments.text: fixed bug

title: call niri msg outputs json output and capture
description: Use Rust's std::process::Command system to invoke calls out to the system shell. In this ticket, we want to call 'niri msg --json outputs' (note the --json flag, this is needed for you to read the output as structured data) and capture what the command returns in STDOUT. It should be parsed, via serde_json, into instances of the data model niri outputs data model. Check the README.md file for examples of the niri msg --json outputs JSON format. Write tests for this as well.

title: Combine Outputs and Niri Outputs screen
description: the last TUI block displayed, Niri Outputs, should have its contents rolled into the first TUI block, Outputs. Each line, which presently corresponds to an output, should have potential labels that say 'DETECTED' or 'CONFIGURED' in different, bright colors. It would be possible to have both labels. You have the 'DETECTED' label if your output entry appears in the Niri Outputs screen/data model. You may ALSO or INSTEAD have  The label 'CONFIGURED' . The labels should appear before the rest of the text in the TUI describing the output entry. This also means that the two data structures should be merged. Or perhaps there should be a single structure for both entry that includes the checkbox. If there are duplicates, by some metric, then they should merge into a single outputs entry with both flags checked to 'true'. Please ask any questions if you need more guidance. If I, the SXC give you more guidance, append it to this description.
close_reason: required manual intervention from human; got task completed
